title: LLM practical decoder proxy
hypothesis: A decoder-style proxy with active-token batches and larger KV-context score dimensions can determine whether practical KV-context pressure changes scheduler or softmax bottleneck evidence before opening a softmax datapath proposal.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the first practical decoder-style proxy expose scheduler, softmax, or KV-context pressure that was absent from the attention-tail stress ladder?
include: 4-6 repeated non-terminal Softmax episodes per model path
existing fp16_nm1 and fp16_nm2 architecture points
exclude: new softmax datapath changes in this proposal
claiming dataset-backed decoder quality from graph-proxy evidence

expected_benefit: adds KV-context pressure to the LLM benchmark ladder without waiting for a full decoder export
prevents softmax architecture work from being launched on tail-only evidence
links campaign-level scheduler/PPA evidence to the existing decoder-quality contract
risks: KV-context pressure is represented through score dimensions and metadata rather than a true KV-cache memory subsystem
the campaign can still report no scheduler pressure, which would push the next step toward dataset-backed decoder execution rather than architecture changes
